title: fresh
text: FWA-Referenced Spatial Hydrology. Stream network-aware spatial operations via direct SQL fwapg bcfishpass. Snap points streams, delineate watersheds, query fish observations, fetch network-referenced features BC Freshwater Atlas.

title: fresh
text: Repository: NewGraphEnvironment/fresh Primary Language: R (package) Version: 0.1.0 License: MIT

title: fresh
text: fresh data layer family packages: fresh — FWA-referenced spatial hydrology (package) flooded — floodplain delineation DEM stream network drift — land cover change detection within floodplains fly — airphoto footprint estimation coverage selection diggs — BC Historic Airphoto Explorer (interactive Shiny app) Pipeline: fresh (network data) → flooded (delineate) → drift (classify)

title: fresh
code: R/   fresh-package.R            — package-level doc, imports   frs_db_conn.R              — DB connection via PG_*_SHARE env vars   frs_db_query.R             — execute SQL, return sf   frs_network.R              — unified multi-table network traversal   frs_network_upstream.R     — upstream network query (ltree)   frs_network_downstream.R   — downstream network query (ltree)   frs_network_prune.R        — prune network to measure range   frs_waterbody_network.R    — lakes/wetlands by waterbody key   frs_point_snap.R           — snap points to nearest stream   frs_point_locate.R         — locate point on stream network   frs_stream_fetch.R         — fetch stream segments   frs_lake_fetch.R           — fetch lakes   frs_wetland_fetch.R        — fetch wetlands   frs_fish_obs.R             — fish observation queries   frs_fish_habitat.R         — habitat model queries   frs_order_filter.R         — filter by stream order   utils.R                    — internal helpers tests/testthat/              — unit tests for each function inst/extdata/                — cached vignette data (subbasin_data.rds) data-raw/                    — hex sticker generation vignettes/                   — .Rmd.orig source, .Rmd pre-knitted output

title: fresh
text: exported functions prefixed frs_, named noun-first: frs_stream_fetch(), frs_point_snap(), etc. frs_network() main workhorse — unified multi-table traversal network subtraction via upstream_measure Direct SQL via DBI/RPostgres fwapg/bcfishpass PostgreSQL Returns sf objects spatial results Requires PostgreSQL fwapg, bcfishpass, bcfishobs extensions Smart defaults: functions detect context (e.g. auto-detect species bcfishpass) Vignette data cached inst/extdata/ update_gis YAML param (FALSE = cached, TRUE = live DB)

title: fresh
text: Runtime: DBI, RPostgres, sf Suggests: tmap (>= 4.0), gq, bookdown, knitr, rmarkdown Database: PostgreSQL fwapg + bcfishpass + bcfishobs Connection: SSH tunnel remote instance local; see db-newgraph skill

title: fresh
text: Checklist effectively running Claude Code agent teams. Agent teams coordinate multiple Claude Code instances working parallel shared tasks inter-agent messaging. Source: code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-teams Status: Experimental. Disabled default.

title: fresh
text: Enable feature flag settings.json environment: Choose display mode — -process (default, terminal) split panes (requires tmux iTerm2). Set teammateMode settings.json pass --teammate-mode. Install tmux using split panes (brew install tmux). supported VS Code terminal, Windows Terminal, Ghostty. Pre-approve common permissions permission settings spawning teammates — permission prompts bubble lead create friction.
code: { "env": { "CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS": "1" } }

title: fresh
text: Use agent teams teammates need talk — research debates, competing hypotheses, cross-layer coordination. Use subagents just need focused workers report back results. Good fit: parallel code review (security + performance + tests), investigating competing bug hypotheses, new modules don’t share files, research multiple angles. Bad fit: sequential tasks, edits file, simple work coordination overhead exceeds benefit.

title: fresh
text: Give enough context spawn prompt — teammates don’t inherit lead’s conversation history. Include file paths, architecture context, specific focus areas. Size tasks right — small = overhead wasted, large = risk divergence. Aim self-contained units clear deliverables. 5-6 tasks per teammate keeps everyone productive. Specify models explicitly want cost control (e.g., “Use Sonnet teammate”). Ensure teammates different files — two teammates editing file leads overwrites.

title: fresh
text: Use delegate mode (Shift+Tab) prevent lead implementing tasks instead waiting teammates. Monitor steer — check progress, redirect approaches aren’t working. Don’t let team run unattended long. Message teammates directly — Shift+/-process mode, click pane split mode. Give additional instructions ask follow-ups. lead starts coding instead delegating, tell : “Wait teammates complete tasks proceeding.”

title: fresh
text: Require plan approval risky work — teammates plan read-mode lead approves. Give criteria: “approve plans include test coverage.” TeammateIdle — exit code 2 sends feedback keeps teammate working TaskCompleted — exit code 2 prevents completion sends feedback

title: fresh
text: Shut teammates first — ask lead: “Ask researcher teammate shut .” Teammates finish current action stopping. clean team — tell lead: “Clean team.” removes shared resources. Cleanup fails teammates still running. Always clean via lead — teammates never run cleanup (team context may resolve correctly). Check orphaned tmux sessions cleanup:
code: tmux ls tmux kill-session -t <session-name>

title: fresh
text: session resumption — /resume /rewind don’t restore -process teammates. resuming, tell lead spawn new teammates. Task status can lag — teammates sometimes fail mark tasks complete, blocking dependents. Manually check update stuck. One team per session — clean starting new team. nested teams — lead can manage team. Lead fixed — can’t promote teammate transfer leadership. Permissions set spawn — teammates inherit lead’s mode. Change individually spawning needed.

title: fresh
text: Standards bookdown report projects across New Graph Environment.

title: fresh
text: canonical references. Child repos inherit structure patterns. mybookdown-template — General-purpose bookdown starter fish_passage_template_reporting — Fish passage reporting template doubt, match template . template production repos disagree, production wins — update template.

title: fresh
code: project/ ├── index.Rmd                # Master config, YAML params, setup chunks ├── _bookdown.yml            # book_filename, output_dir: "docs" ├── _output.yml              # Gitbook, pagedown, pdf_book config ├── 0100-intro.Rmd           # Chapter numbering: 4-digit, 100s increment ├── 0200-background.Rmd ├── 0300-methods.Rmd ├── 0400-results.Rmd ├── 0500-*.Rmd               # Discussion/recommendations ├── 0800-appendix-*.Rmd      # Appendices (site-specific in fish passage) ├── 2000-references.Rmd      # Auto-generated from .bib ├── 2090-report-change-log.Rmd  # Auto-generated from NEWS.md ├── 2100-session-info.Rmd    # Reproducibility ├── NEWS.md                  # Changelog (semantic versioning) ├── scripts/ │   ├── packages.R           # Package loading (renv-managed) │   ├── functions.R          # Project-specific functions │   ├── staticimports.R      # Auto-generated from staticimports pkg │   ├── setup_docs.R         # Build helper │   └── run.R                # Local build (gitbook + PDF) ├── fig/                     # Figures (organized by chapter or type) ├── data/                    # Project data ├── docs/                    # Rendered output (GitHub Pages) ├── renv.lock                # Locked dependencies └── .Rprofile                # Activates renv

title: fresh
text: Every index.Rmd follows setup sequence. Order matters. Responsive settings output format:
code: # 1. Gitbook vs PDF switch gitbook_on <- TRUE  # 2. Knitr options knitr::opts_chunk$set(   echo = identical(gitbook_on, TRUE),  # Show code only in gitbook   message = FALSE, warning = FALSE,   dpi = 60, out.width = "100%" ) options(scipen = 999) options(knitr.kable.NA = '--') options(knitr.kable.NAN = '--')  # 3. Source in order: packages → static imports → functions → data source('scripts/packages.R') source('scripts/staticimports.R') source('scripts/functions.R') # Gitbook photo_width <- "100%"; font_set <- 11  # PDF (paged.js) photo_width <- "80%"; font_set <- 9

title: fresh
text: Parameters live index.Rmd frontmatter (separate file). Child repos override editing values. Fish passage repos add project-specific params (project_region, model_species, wsg_code, update flags forms). project-specific — don’t add general template.
code: params:   repo_url: 'https://github.com/NewGraphEnvironment/repo_name'   report_url: 'https://www.newgraphenvironment.com/repo_name/'   update_packages: FALSE   update_bib: TRUE   gitbook_on: TRUE

title: fresh
text: Embed context purpose chunk names. principle universal; codes project-specific. Pattern: {type}-{system}-{description}

title: fresh
text: Bookdown auto-prepends fig: tab: chunk names. Tables: Table \@ref(tab:chunk-name) Figures: Figure \@ref(fig:chunk-name) fig: tab: prefix chunk label — bookdown adds .

title: fresh
text: Interactive tables (DT) can’t use standard bookdown captions. Use my_tab_caption() function staticimports.R. Pattern: Separate -cap chunk table chunk. my_tab_caption() auto-grabs chunk label via knitr::opts_current$get()$label wraps HTML caption tags bookdown can cross-reference.
code: # Caption chunk — must use results="asis" {r tab-sites-sum-cap, results="asis"} my_caption <- "Summary of fish passage assessment procedures." my_tab_caption() # Table chunk — renders the DT {r tab-sites-sum} data |> my_dt_table(page_length = 20, cols_freeze_left = 0)

title: fresh
text: Separate prep chunk (find file) display chunk (render ).
code: # Prep — find the photo {r photo-196332-01-prep} my_photo1 <- fpr::fpr_photo_pull_by_str(str_to_pull = 'ds_typical_1_') my_caption1 <- paste0('Typical habitat downstream of PSCIS crossing ', my_site, '.') # Gitbook — full width {r photo-196332-01, fig.cap=my_caption1, out.width=photo_width, eval=gitbook_on} knitr::include_graphics(my_photo1) # PDF — side by side with 1% spacer {r photo-196332-d01, fig.show="hold", out.width=c("49.5%","1%","49.5%"), eval=identical(gitbook_on, FALSE)} knitr::include_graphics(my_photo1) knitr::include_graphics("fig/pixel.png") knitr::include_graphics(my_photo2)

title: fresh
text: references.bib auto-generated — never edit manually. build, rbbt::bbt_write_bib() scans .Rmd files @citekey references, pulls BibTeX Zotero’s Better BibTeX, overwrites references.bib. manual additions lost next build. add reference: add shared Zotero group library, use BBT citation key (@key) .Rmd text, build. rbbt handles rest. update_bib: FALSE params, build uses existing references.bib without regenerating — useful offline builds CI Zotero isn’t running. Auto-generate package citations: Use nocite: YAML include references cited text.
code: bibliography: "`r rbbt::bbt_write_bib('references.bib', overwrite = TRUE)`" biblio-style: apalike link-citations: no knitr::write_bib(c(.packages(), 'bookdown', 'knitr', 'rmarkdown'), 'packages.bib')

title: fresh
text: index.Rmd contains two separate front-matter sections setup chunks:

title: fresh
text: Three parts, order: Personal connection land (template-level, across reports): > New Graph Environment, understand well-inseparable health land waters work within. care ecosystems, care communities connected . relationship metaphorical — foundation practice. Colonial acknowledgement (template-level): > Modern civilization long journey ahead acknowledge address historic ongoing impacts colonialism… Territorial acknowledgement (project-specific, must edited per report): Name Nations, governance systems, watersheds, species relevant project. use generic office-location acknowledgement — tie territory work happens. See Wedzin Kwa chinook example pattern. Funding partners (project-specific).

title: fresh
text: use # heading disclosure — creates separate chapter page gitbook. Instead, add YAML date: field renders title block: Wording principle: accurate LLM . assisted drafting synthesis — make scientific interpretations conclusions. say “independently verified authors” (redundant) attribute “ecological assessments” LLM. regulatory/EGBC-stamped work, use extended disclaimer soul/research/20260212_ai_disclosure_research.md. See NewGraphEnvironment/mybookdown-template#89.
code: date: |  |  | Version X.X.X DRAFT `r format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-%m-%d")`  |  | *Claude Sonnet 4.6 (Anthropic) assisted with literature synthesis, drafting, and technical writing. All scientific interpretation, data analysis, and conclusions are the responsibility of the authors.*

title: fresh
text: single boolean gitbook_on controls output format throughout.
code: # Show only in gitbook {r map-interactive, eval=gitbook_on}  # Show only in PDF {r fig-print-only, eval=identical(gitbook_on, FALSE)}  # Conditional inline content `r if(identical(gitbook_on, FALSE)) knitr::asis_output("This report is available online...")`  # Page breaks for PDF only `r if(gitbook_on){knitr::asis_output("")} else knitr::asis_output("\\pagebreak")`

title: fresh
text: Reports use MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH versioning NEWS.md changelog. Version index.Rmd YAML: NEWS.md format: Auto-append appendix via my_news_to_appendix() staticimports.R: Convention: - Bump version index.Rmd add NEWS entry every commit main changes report content - Tag releases: git tag -v1.1.0 -m "v1.1.0: Brief description" - MAJOR: structural changes, new chapters, methodology changes - MINOR: new content, figures, tables, discussion sections - PATCH: prose fixes, corrections, formatting
code: date: |  |  | Version 1.1.0 DRAFT `r format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-%m-%d")` ## 1.1.0 (2026-02-17)  - Add feature X - Fix issue Y ([Issue #N](https://github.com/Org/repo/issues/N)) news_to_appendix(md_name = "NEWS.md", rmd_name = "2090-report-change-log.Rmd")

title: fresh
text: Always use ngr::ngr_str_viewer_cog() — never hardcode viewer iframes. function includes cache-busting ?v= parameter. Bump v function default viewer.html breaking changes.
code: knitr::asis_output(ngr::ngr_str_viewer_cog("https://bucket.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/ortho.tif"))

title: fresh
text: Use renv reproducible package management: - .Rprofile activates renv startup - renv::restore() installs lockfile - renv::snapshot() updates lockfile adding packages - Use pak::pak("pkg") install (install.packages)

title: fresh
text: Production repos (2024-2025) drifted templates areas. working child repo, match repo , template: Script naming 02_reporting/ — older repos use tables.R, 0165-read-sqlite.R; newer repos use numbered 0130-tables.R. Follow repo ’re . Removed packages — elevatr, rayshader, arrow removed production still template. staticimports::import() call — repos skip source staticimports.R directly. Hardcoded vs parameterized years — older repos hardcode years file paths; newer repos use params$project_year. Prefer parameterized.

title: fresh
text: Use gq package shared layer symbology. Never hardcode hex color values registry style exists. Core pattern: reg$layers$lake, reg$layers$road, reg$layers$bec_zone, etc.
code: library(gq) reg <- gq_reg_main()  # load once per script — 51+ layers

title: fresh
text: project-specific layers main registry, use hand-curated CSV merge: Install: pak::pak("NewGraphEnvironment/gq")
code: reg <- gq_reg_merge(gq_reg_main(), gq_reg_read_csv("path/to/custom.csv"))

title: fresh
text: sf_use_s2(FALSE) top every mapping script Compute area simplify SQL map title — title belongs report caption Legend least-important terrain — swap legend logo sides reduces AOI occlusion. fixed convention side. Four-corner rule — legend, logo, scale bar, keymap get corner. Never stack two quadrant. Bbox must match canvas aspect ratio — compute ratio geographic extents page dimensions. Mismatch causes white space bands. Consistent element--frame spacing — inset elements visually equal margins frame edge Map fills frame — basemap extends edge--edge, dead bands. Use near-zero inner.margins outer.margins. Suppress auto-legends — build manual ones registry values CAPS labels appear larger — use title case legend labels (gq gq_tmap_classes() handles automatically via to_title() fallback)

title: fresh
text: Read PNG check showing anyone: Correct polygon/study area shown? (verify source data, just bbox) Map fills page? (white/black bands) Keymap inside frame spacing edge? element overlap? (corner) Legend least-important terrain? Consistent spacing across elements? Scale bar breaks appropriate extent? See cartography skill full reference: basemap blending, BC spatial data queries, label hierarchy, mapgl gotchas, worked examples.

title: fresh
text: Use drift flooded together riparian land cover change analysis. flooded delineates floodplain extents DEMs stream networks; drift tracks ’s changing inside time. Pipeline: Class colors come drift’s shipped class tables (IO LULC, ESA WorldCover) production COGs S3, dft_map_interactive() serves tiles via titiler — set options(drift.titiler_url = "...") See drift vignette worked example (Neexdzii Kwa floodplain, 2017-2023)
code: # 1. Delineate floodplain AOI (flooded) valleys <- flooded::fl_valley_confine(dem, streams)  # 2. Fetch, classify, summarize (drift) rasters   <- drift::dft_stac_fetch(aoi, source = "io-lulc", years = c(2017, 2020, 2023)) classified <- drift::dft_rast_classify(rasters, source = "io-lulc") summary    <- drift::dft_rast_summarize(classified, unit = "ha")  # 3. Interactive map with layer toggle drift::dft_map_interactive(classified, aoi = aoi)

title: fresh
text: Structured checklist reviewing diffs commit. Used /code-check. Add new checks bug class discovered — compound time.

title: fresh
text: Variables double-quoted strings containing single quotes break value ' "echo '${VAR}'" — VAR contains ', shell syntax breaks Use printf '%s\n' "$VAR" | command pipe values safely Heredocs: unquoted <<EOF expands variables locally, <<'EOF' — know need

title: fresh
text: Hardcoded absolute paths (/Users/airvine/...) break users Use REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/<relative>" && pwd)" moving scripts, verify ../ depth still resolves correctly Usage comments match actual script location

title: fresh
text: || true hides real errors — failure actually safe ignore? Empty variable destructive operation (rm, destroy) — add guard: [ -n "$VAR" ] || exit 1 grep returning empty silently — downstream commands get empty input

title: fresh
text: Secrets passed command-line args visible ps aux Use env files, stdin pipes, temp files chmod 600 instead

title: fresh
text: Must pure ASCII — em dashes, curly quotes, arrows cause silent parse failure Check : perl -ne 'print "$.: $_" /[^\x00-\x7F]/' file.yaml

title: fresh
text: cloud-init clean causes full re-provisioning next boot — almost never want snapshot Use tailscale logout tailscale snapshot (deregister vs disconnect)

title: fresh
text: Secrets rendered via templatefile() readable 169.254.169.254 metadata endpoint Acceptable ephemeral machines, document tradeoff

title: fresh
text: Parsing tofu state show text output fragile — use tofu output instead Missing outputs scripts need — add main.tf Snapshot/image IDs tfvars deleting snapshot — stale reference

title: fresh
text: Validate resource IDs destroy: [ -n "$ID" ] || exit 1 tofu destroy without -target destroys everything including reserved IPs Snapshot ID extraction: use --resource droplet grep -F exact match

title: fresh
text: .tfvars must gitignored (contains tokens, passwords) .tfvars.example variables empty/placeholder values Sensitive variables need sensitive = true variables.tf

title: fresh
text: 0.0.0.0/0 SSH world-open — document intentional access gated Tailscale, say explicitly

title: fresh
text: Passwords special chars (', ", $, !) break naive shell quoting printf '%q' escapes values shell safety Temp files secrets: create chmod 600, delete use

title: fresh
text: pak stops first unresolvable package — subsequent packages skipped Removed CRAN packages (like leaflet.extras) must move GitHub source PPPM binaries may lag hours behind new CRAN releases

title: fresh
text: Branch pins (pkg@branch) reproducible — document used Pinned download URLs (RStudio .deb) go stale — document update

title: fresh
text: Every changed line trace task — drive-cleanups New files need: gitignored sensitive? executable scripts? Deleted files: check references files, docs, READMEs

title: fresh
text: Moving/renaming scripts: update CLAUDE.md, READMEs, usage comments New variables: update .tfvars.example New workflows: update relevant README

title: fresh
text: Standards external communications across New Graph Environment. compost working repo email drafts, scripts, contact management, Gmail utilities. conventions capture universal principles; compost implementation details.

title: fresh
text: Three levels. Default casual unless context dictates otherwise. Collaborative, directive. Acknowledge constraints: Avoid: “Work makes sense lab” Better: “’re able work fits schedule really helpful”

title: fresh
text: Draft markdown, convert HTML send time via gmailr. See compost script templates, OAuth setup, search_gmail.R. File naming: YYYYMMDD_recipient_topic_draft.md + YYYYMMDD_recipient_topic.R Key gotchas (documented detail compost): - Gmail strips <style> blocks — use inline styles tables - gm_create_draft() support thread_id — gm_send_message() can reply threads. Drafts land outside conversation. - Always use test_mode create_draft variables safe workflows

title: fresh
text: Never manually type data tables — generate programmatically source files Link canonical sources (GitHub repos, public reports) rather embedding raw data Provide CSV Excel sharing tabular data Document ID codes — using compressed IDs (e.g., id_lab), include reference sheet recipients can decode

title: fresh
text: Internal QA info (blanks, control samples, calibration data) Internal tracking codes SRED references Draft status revision history Internal project management details Keep client-facing communications focused deliverables technical content.

title: fresh
text:
HTML emails, use
tags lines.
code: Al Irvine B.Sc., R.P.Bio. New Graph Environment Ltd.  Cell: 250-777-1518 Email: al@newgraphenvironment.com Website: www.newgraphenvironment.com

title: fresh
text: Behavioral guidelines reduce common LLM coding mistakes. Merge project-specific instructions needed. Tradeoff: guidelines bias toward caution speed. trivial tasks, use judgment.

title: fresh
text: Don’t assume. Don’t hide confusion. Surface tradeoffs. implementing: - State assumptions explicitly. uncertain, ask. - multiple interpretations exist, present - don’t pick silently. - simpler approach exists, say . Push back warranted. - something unclear, stop. Name ’s confusing. Ask.

title: fresh
text: Minimum code solves problem. Nothing speculative. features beyond asked. abstractions single-use code. “flexibility” “configurability” wasn’t requested. error handling impossible scenarios. write 200 lines 50, rewrite . Ask : “senior engineer say overcomplicated?” yes, simplify.

title: fresh
text: Touch must. Clean mess. editing existing code: - Don’t “improve” adjacent code, comments, formatting. - Don’t refactor things aren’t broken. - Match existing style, even ’d differently. - notice unrelated dead code, mention - don’t delete . changes create orphans: - Remove imports/variables/functions changes made unused. - Don’t remove pre-existing dead code unless asked. test: Every changed line trace directly user’s request.

title: fresh
text: Define success criteria. Loop verified. Transform tasks verifiable goals: - “Add validation” → “Write tests invalid inputs, make pass” - “Fix bug” → “Write test reproduces , make pass” - “Refactor X” → “Ensure tests pass ” multi-step tasks, state brief plan: Strong success criteria let loop independently. Weak criteria (“make work”) require constant clarification. guidelines working : fewer unnecessary changes diffs, fewer rewrites due overcomplication, clarifying questions come implementation rather mistakes.
code: 1. [Step] → verify: [check] 2. [Step] → verify: [check] 3. [Step] → verify: [check]

title: fresh
text: Core patterns professional, efficient workflows across New Graph Environment repositories.

title: fresh
text: Five repos form governance operations layer across New Graph Environment work: Adaptive management: Conventions evolve real project work, theory. pattern learned refined project work, propagate back soul projects benefit. /claude-md-init skill builds project’s CLAUDE.md soul conventions. Cross-references: sred-2025-2026 tracks R&D activities across repos. Compost cross-cuts projects centralized communications workflow — email drafts, contact registry, tone guidelines live copied individual project communications/ folders needed.

title: fresh
text: Check duplicates: gh issue list --state open --search "<keywords>" – search creating Link SRED: work involves infrastructure, R&D, tooling, performance benchmarking, add Relates NewGraphEnvironment/sred-2025-2026#N (match repo name SRED issue title) One issue, one concern. Keep focused.

title: fresh
text: Write issues clear technical focus: Use normal technical language titles descriptions Focus problem solution approach Add tracking links end (e.g., Relates Owner/repo#N) Issue body structure:
code: ## Problem <what's wrong or missing>  ## Proposed Solution <approach>  Relates to #<local> Relates to NewGraphEnvironment/sred-2025-2026#<N>

what: GitHub Issue Creation - Always Use Files
title: fresh
text: gh issue create command heredoc syntax fails repeatedly EOF errors. ALWAYS use --body-file:
code: cat > /tmp/issue_body.md << 'EOF' ## Problem ...  ## Proposed Solution ... EOF  gh issue create --title "Brief technical title" --body-file /tmp/issue_body.md

title: fresh
text: : Close issues via commit messages. commit closure documentation. DON’T: Close issues gh issue close. breaks audit trail — ’s linked diff showing changed. Fixes #N Closes #N — auto-closes links commit issue Relates #N — partial progress, close Always close issues work complete. Don’t leave stale open issues.
code: Fix broken DEM path in loading pipeline  Update hardcoded path to use config-driven resolution.  Fixes #20 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: fresh
text: Write clear, informative commit messages: commit: - Logical, atomic units work - Working state (tests pass) - Clear description changes avoid: - “WIP” “temp” commits main branch - Combining unrelated changes - Vague messages like “fixes” “updates”
code: Brief description (50 chars or less)  Detailed explanation of changes and impact.  Fixes #<issue> (or Relates to #<issue>) Relates to NewGraphEnvironment/sred-2025-2026#<N>  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: fresh
text: Rules learned real project sessions. apply across repos. Cite primary sources — review paper references older study, trace back original cite . Don’t attribute findings review original exists. Install missing packages, don’t workaround — package needed, ask user install (e.g. pak::pak("pkg")). Don’t write degraded fallback code avoid dependency. Never hardcode extractable data — coordinates, station names, metadata can pulled API database runtime, . Don’t hardcode values programmatic source. Close issues via commits, gh issue close — see Closing Issues .

title: fresh
text: Pattern: noun_verb-detail – noun first, verb second across naming: Scripts logs live together: scripts/<module>/logs/

title: fresh
text: Projects = daily cross-repo tracking (always add relevant project) Milestones = iteration boundaries (release/claim prep) Don’t double-track unless ’s reason

title: fresh
text: Automated PR review interactive Claude mentions using Claude Code GitHub Action.

title: fresh
text: Sets CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN repo secret automatically Uses Max subscription (flat $200/mo, per-token) Choose mode: review (auto PR review + @claude mentions) mention (@claude ) Pushes workflow template .github/workflows/claude.yml Ensure CLAUDE.md exists repo — action reads automatically. Use /claude-md-init set missing.

title: fresh
text: every PR open/update, Claude reviews diff posts: - Inline comments specific lines issues - Summary comment overall assessment - Uses Haiku speed cost (~$0.01-0.05 per review)

title: fresh
text: Comment @claude <request> issue PR. Claude can: - Answer questions codebase - Create branches open PRs issue descriptions - Fix code push commits - Run slash commands: @claude /review - Uses Sonnet deeper reasoning

title: fresh
text: override, edit claude_args workflow: Available models (use exact IDs): - claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 — cheapest, fastest - claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 — balanced - claude-opus-4-6 — capable, expensive
code: claude_args: |   --model claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929

title: fresh
code: @claude what does the validate_crossing function do? @claude fix the failing lintr checks and open a PR @claude /review — focus on the database query performance @claude add tests for the new helper functions in R/utils.R

what: Bot Workflow (for OpenClaw bots opening PRs)

text: bot opens PR, : Push PR wait Claude review action complete Read review comments via gh pr view --comments Fix -brainers automatically: lint issues, typos, missing imports Ask humans judgment calls: architecture, logic changes, scope Push fix commits re-trigger review Stop 3 rounds — still failing, tag human reviewer

text: Use --max-turns cap iterations cost.

text: tidyverse style guide: snake_case, pipe operators (|> %>%) Match existing patterns codebase Use pak package installation (install.packages)

title: fresh
text: Follow R Packages (2e) conventions: - R/ functions, tests/testthat/ tests, man/ docs - DESCRIPTION proper fields (Title, Description, Authors@R) - DESCRIPTION URL field: include GitHub repo pkgdown site pkgdown links correctly (e.g., URL: https://github.com/OWNER/PKG,   https://owner.github.io/PKG/) - NAMESPACE managed roxygen2 (#' @export, #' @import, #' @importFrom) - Never edit NAMESPACE man/ hand

title: fresh
text: exported function gets R file test file: - R/fl_mask.R → tests/testthat/test-fl_mask.R - Commit function tests together - Use Fixes #N commit message close corresponding issue

title: fresh
text: File GitHub issue function building . creates traceable record planned, built, verified.

title: fresh
text: new packages major features, work branch merge via PR: gives one PR contains commits — single SRED cross-reference covers entire body work. Individual commits within branch close respective function issues Fixes #N.
code: main ← scaffold-branch (PR closes with "Relates to NewGraphEnvironment/sred-2025-2026#N")

title: fresh
text: Close function issues via commit messages (gh issue close) diff linked:
code: Add fl_mask() with threshold and operator support  Fixes #3 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: fresh
text: Use testthat 3e (Config/testthat/edition: 3 DESCRIPTION) Run devtools::test() committing Test files mirror source: R/utils.R -> tests/testthat/test-utils.R Test edge cases potential failures, just happy paths Tests must pass closing function’s issue

what: Runnable examples on every exported function
title: fresh
text: Examples users discover function . must: - Actually run — \dontrun{} unless external resources required - Use bundled test data via system.file() work anyone - Show function useful — just runs, produces ’d use - Use qualified names non-exported dependencies (terra::rast(), sf::st_read()) since examples run user’s environment

title: fresh
text: least one vignette showing full pipeline real data: - Demonstrates package solving actual problem end--end - Uses bundled test data (committed inst/testdata/) - Hosted pkgdown users can read without installing

title: fresh
text: Created via script data-raw/ documents exactly data produced (database queries, spatial crops, etc.) Committed inst/testdata/ — small enough ship package Used tests, examples, vignettes — one dataset, three purposes

title: fresh
text: roxygen2 exported functions @import @importFrom package-level doc (R/<pkg>-package.R) populate NAMESPACE — don’t rely :: everywhere function bodies pkgdown site public packages _pkgdown.yml (bootstrap 5) GitHub Action pkgdown (usethis::use_github_action("pkgdown"))

title: fresh
text: Run lintr::lint_package() committing R package code. Fix warnings — every lint worth fixing.

title: fresh
text: 120 char line length (default 80 strict data pipelines) Allow dotted.case (common base R legacy code) Suppress commented code lints (exploratory R scripts often commented alternatives) Exclude renv directory entirely
code: linters: linters_with_defaults(     line_length_linter(120),     object_name_linter(styles = c("snake_case", "dotted.case")),     commented_code_linter = NULL   ) exclusions: list(     "renv" = list(linters = "all")   )

title: fresh
text: Minimize Imports — use Suggests packages needed tests/vignettes Pin versions breaking changes known Prefer packages already tidyverse ecosystem

title: fresh
text: First release: one line (e.g., “Initial release. Brief description.”) Later releases: describe changed , function--function. Link pkgdown reference page details — don’t duplicate . Don’t list every function; pkgdown reference page single source truth ’s package. Bump version DESCRIPTION (e.g., 0.0.0.9000 → 0.1.0) Commit “Release vX.Y.Z” Tag: git tag vX.Y.Z && git push && git push --tags

title: fresh
text: Protect main deletion force pushes. Admin bypasses can push directly:
code: gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/rulesets --method POST --input - <<'EOF' {   "name": "Protect main",   "target": "branch",   "enforcement": "active",   "bypass_actors": [     { "actor_id": 5, "actor_type": "RepositoryRole", "bypass_mode": "always" }   ],   "conditions": { "ref_name": { "include": ["refs/heads/main"], "exclude": [] } },   "rules": [ { "type": "deletion" }, { "type": "non_fast_forward" } ] } EOF

title: fresh
text: usethis::create_package(".") usethis::use_mit_license("New Graph Environment Ltd.") usethis::use_testthat(edition = 3) usethis::use_pkgdown() usethis::use_github_action("pkgdown") usethis::use_directory("dev") — reproducible setup script usethis::use_directory("data-raw") — data generation scripts Hex sticker via hexSticker (see data-raw/make_hexsticker.R) Set GitHub Pages serve gh-pages branch

title: fresh
text: Keep dev/dev.R file documents every setup step. idempotent — run interactively. reproducible recipe package scaffold.

title: fresh
text: Keep README lean: - Hex sticker, one-line description, install, example showing ’s useful - Link pkgdown vignette function reference — don’t duplicate - Don’t maintain function table — ’s just another thing keep updated pkgdown’s reference page single source truth

title: fresh
text: LLM assistant modifies R package code: 1. Run lintr::lint_package() — fix issues committing 2. Run devtools::test() — ensure tests pass 3. Run devtools::document() roxygen comments changed 4. Check devtools::check() passes releases

title: fresh
text: references flow Claude Code, Zotero, technical writing New Graph Environment.

title: fresh
text: Three tools, different purposes. Use right one. Citation keys vs item keys: Citation keys (like irvine2020ParsnipRiver) come Better BibTeX. Item keys (like K7WALMSY) native Zotero. MCP works item keys. /zotero-lookup bridges citation keys item data. BBT citation key storage: Feb 2025+, BBT stores citation keys citationKey field directly zotero.sqlite (via Zotero’s item data system), separate BBT database. old better-bibtex.sqlite better-bibtex.migrated files stale longer updated. Query citation keys : SELECT idv.value items JOIN itemData id .itemID = id.itemID JOIN itemDataValues idv id.valueID = idv.valueID JOIN fields f id.fieldID = f.fieldID f.fieldName = 'citationKey'.

title: fresh
text: research turns reference: - DOI available: Tell user — Zotero’s magic wand (DOI lookup) fastest path - ResearchGate link: Flag user manual check — programmatic fetch blocked (403), full text often - BC gov report: Search ACAT, .gov.bc.ca library, EIRS viewer - Paywalled: Note , move . Don’t waste time trying bypass.

title: fresh
text: Preferred order: 1. DOI magic wand Zotero UI (fastest, complete metadata) 2. saveItems via /zotero-api (good batch creation structured data) 3. JS console script group library (connector can’t target right collection) Collection targeting: saveItems drops items whatever collection selected Zotero’s UI. Always confirm user calling .

title: fresh
text: saveItems attachments silently fail. Don’t use . Instead: Download curl (see /zotero-api skill source-specific patterns) Attach via item_attach_pdf.js Zotero JS console Verify attachment exists via MCP: zotero_get_item_children

title: fresh
text: manual adds, confirm via MCP: - zotero_search_items — find title - zotero_get_item_metadata — check fields complete - zotero_get_item_children — confirm PDF attached

title: fresh
text: duplicates created (common saveItems retries): - Run collection_dedup.js via Zotero JS console - keeps copy attachments, trashes rest

title: fresh
text: rbbt pulls BBT, syncs Zotero. Edit references Zotero → rebuild report → bibliography updates. Library targeting: rbbt must know Zotero library search. set globally ~/.Rprofile: Without option, rbbt searches personal library (libraryID 1) won’t find group library references. library IDs map Zotero’s internal numbering — use /zotero-lookup SELECT DISTINCT libraryID citationkey BBT database discover available libraries.
code: # index.Rmd — dynamic bib from Zotero via Better BibTeX bibliography: "`r rbbt::bbt_write_bib('references.bib', overwrite = TRUE)`" # default library — NewGraphEnvironment group (libraryID 9, group 4733734) options(rbbt.default.library_id = 9)

title: fresh
text: [@key2020] — parenthetical: (Author 2020) @key2020 — narrative: Author (2020) [@key1; @key2] — multiple nocite: YAML — include uncited references

title: fresh
text: review paper references older study, trace back original cite . Don’t attribute findings review original exists. (See LLM Agent Conventions newgraph.md.) original unavailable (paywalled, print, can’t locate): use secondary citation format prose include bib entries sources: Smith et al. (2003; cited Doctor 2022) found … @smith2003 @doctor2022 go .bib file. reader can track original . Flag incomplete metadata primary entry — ’s better partial reference none .

title: fresh
text: need PDF obvious URL doesn’t work: DOI resolver → publisher site (often OA link) Europe PMC (europepmc.org/backend/ptpmcrender.fcgi?accid=PMC{ID}&blobtype=pdf) — ncbi blocks curl SciELO — needs User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 header ResearchGate — flag user manual download Semantic Scholar — sometimes OA links Ask user institutional access Always verify downloads: file paper.pdf say “PDF document”, HTML.

title: fresh
text: SR&ED (Scientific Research Experimental Development) tracking integrates New Graph Environment’s development workflows.

title: fresh

title: fresh
text: Boast interview guide CRA guidelines: Eligible (systematic investigation overcome technological uncertainty): - Building tools/functions don’t exist standard practice - Prototyping new integrations systems (GIS ↔︎ reporting ↔︎ field collection) - Testing whether approach works documenting /didn’t - Iterating failed approaches new hypotheses eligible (still important document due diligence): - Evaluating existing tools confirm can’t meet requirements - Standard configuration known tools - Routine bug fixes working systems - Writing reports using framework (’s service delivery) test: “try something weren’t sure work, learn something attempt?” yes, ’s likely eligible.

title: fresh
text: Subcontractors eligible SRED reduced rate (~32% vs ~64% T4 employees). Arm’s length, Canadian contractors . work must performed Canada. core SRED work, T4 employees generate roughly 2x credit per dollar spent. Use contractors needed aware credit differential.

title: fresh
text: fiscal year-end, Boast needs: 1. Time records — hours person, tagged iterations (rolex) 2. Technical narrative — attempted, worked, didn’t (commit history, issues, PRs) 3. Financial summary — wages, contractor payments, expenses (rolex + Xero) 4. GitHub evidence — commits, issues, PRs showing systematic investigation (sred-2025-2026 cross-references) better tagging year, less work claim time.

text: Irvine (2026). fresh: FWA-Referenced Spatial Hydrology. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/NewGraphEnvironment/fresh.
code: @Manual{,   title = {fresh: FWA-Referenced Spatial Hydrology},   author = {Allan Irvine},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/NewGraphEnvironment/fresh}, }

title: FWA-Referenced Spatial Hydrology
text: FWA-Referenced Spatial Hydrology Stream network-aware spatial operations via direct SQL fwapg bcfishpass. Snap points streams, delineate watersheds, query fish observations, fetch network-referenced features BC Freshwater Atlas.

title: FWA-Referenced Spatial Hydrology
code: pak::pak("NewGraphEnvironment/fresh")

title: FWA-Referenced Spatial Hydrology
text: fresh requires PostgreSQL following extensions loaded: fwapg — BC Freshwater Atlas PostgreSQL bcfishpass — fish passage habitat modelling bcfishobs — fish observation data Connection configured via PG_*_SHARE environment variables (PG_HOST_SHARE, PG_PORT_SHARE, PG_DB_SHARE, PG_USER_SHARE) passed directly frs_db_conn().

title: FWA-Referenced Spatial Hydrology
text: Query network features two points blue line key using watershed code subtraction — spatial clipping needed: See subbasin vignette full worked example map output.
code: library(fresh)  result <- frs_network(   blue_line_key = 360873822,   downstream_route_measure = 208877,   upstream_measure = 233564,   tables = list(     streams = "whse_basemapping.fwa_stream_networks_sp",     lakes   = "whse_basemapping.fwa_lakes_poly",     fish_obs = "bcfishobs.fiss_fish_obsrvtn_events_vw",     falls   = "bcfishpass.falls_vw"   ) )

title: FWA-Referenced Spatial Hydrology
text: fresh data layer family packages: fresh — fetch query FWA network data (package) flooded — floodplain delineation DEM stream network drift — land cover change detection within floodplains fly — airphoto footprint estimation coverage selection diggs — BC Historic Airphoto Explorer (interactive Shiny app) Pipeline: fresh (network data) → flooded (delineate) → drift (classify)

title: FWA-Referenced Spatial Hydrology
text: MIT

title: fresh: FWA-Referenced Spatial Hydrology — fresh-package
text: Stream network-aware spatial operations via direct SQL fwapg bcfishpass. Snap points streams, delineate watersheds, query fish observations, fetch network-referenced lakes, wetlands, streams BC Freshwater Atlas. Requires PostgreSQL fwapg, bcfishpass, bcfishobs.

title: fresh: FWA-Referenced Spatial Hydrology — fresh-package
text: Maintainer: Allan Irvine al@newgraphenvironment.com (ORCID) contributors: New Graph Environment [copyright holder]

what: Connect to FWA PostgreSQL Database — frs_db_conn
title: Connect to FWA PostgreSQL Database — frs_db_conn
text: Opens connection PostgreSQL database containing fwapg, bcfishpass, bcfishobs. Connection parameters default environment variables matching PG_*_SHARE convention used fpr.

title: Connect to FWA PostgreSQL Database — frs_db_conn
code: frs_db_conn(   dbname = Sys.getenv("PG_DB_SHARE"),   host = Sys.getenv("PG_HOST_SHARE"),   port = Sys.getenv("PG_PORT_SHARE"),   user = Sys.getenv("PG_USER_SHARE"),   password = Sys.getenv("PG_PASS_SHARE") )

title: Connect to FWA PostgreSQL Database — frs_db_conn
text: dbname Database name. Default: Sys.getenv("PG_DB_SHARE"). host Host name. Default: Sys.getenv("PG_HOST_SHARE"). port Port number. Default: Sys.getenv("PG_PORT_SHARE"). user User name. Default: Sys.getenv("PG_USER_SHARE"). password Password. Default: Sys.getenv("PG_PASS_SHARE").

title: Connect to FWA PostgreSQL Database — frs_db_conn
text: DBI::DBIConnection object.

title: Connect to FWA PostgreSQL Database — frs_db_conn
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- frs_db_conn() DBI::dbDisconnect(conn) } # }

title: Query FWA PostgreSQL Database — frs_db_query
text: Connects via frs_db_conn(), executes SQL query, disconnects, returns result. Uses sf::st_read() spatial columns returned sf geometry.

title: Query FWA PostgreSQL Database — frs_db_query
code: frs_db_query(query, ...)

title: Query FWA PostgreSQL Database — frs_db_query
text: query Character. SQL query string. ... Additional arguments passed frs_db_conn().

title: Query FWA PostgreSQL Database — frs_db_query
text: sf data frame (query returns geometry) plain data frame.

title: Query FWA PostgreSQL Database — frs_db_query
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ frs_db_query("SELECT * FROM whse_basemapping.fwa_lakes_poly LIMIT 5") } # }

what: Fetch Modelled Fish Habitat from bcfishpass — frs_fish_habitat
title: Fetch Modelled Fish Habitat from bcfishpass — frs_fish_habitat
text: Query stream segments habitat model outputs bcfishpass table. Filter watershed group /blue line key. Returns segments barrier, access, habitat classification columns.

title: Fetch Modelled Fish Habitat from bcfishpass — frs_fish_habitat
code: frs_fish_habitat(   watershed_group_code = NULL,   blue_line_key = NULL,   table = "bcfishpass.streams_vw",   cols = c("segmented_stream_id", "blue_line_key", "waterbody_key",     "downstream_route_measure", "upstream_area_ha", "gnis_name", "stream_order",     "channel_width", "gradient", "mad_m3s", "watershed_group_code", "wscode",     "localcode", "geom"),   limit = NULL,   ... )

title: Fetch Modelled Fish Habitat from bcfishpass — frs_fish_habitat
text: watershed_group_code Character. Watershed group code. Default NULL. blue_line_key Integer. Blue line key. Default NULL. table Character. Fully qualified table name. Default "bcfishpass.streams_vw". cols Character vector column names select. Default includes commonly used habitat model attributes. limit Integer. Maximum rows return. Default NULL. ... Additional arguments passed frs_db_conn().

title: Fetch Modelled Fish Habitat from bcfishpass — frs_fish_habitat
text: sf data frame stream segments bcfishpass habitat model columns (barriers, access, gradient, channel width, etc.).

Blue line key. Default NULL. table Character. Fully qualified table name. Default "bcfishobs.fiss_fish_obsrvtn_events_vw". cols Character vector column names select. Default includes commonly used observation attributes. limit Integer. Maximum rows return. Default NULL. ... Additional arguments passed frs_db_conn().

text: Retrieve lake polygons FWA lakes table. Filter watershed group code, blue line key, /bounding box.

title: Fetch FWA Lakes — frs_lake_fetch
code: frs_lake_fetch(   watershed_group_code = NULL,   blue_line_key = NULL,   bbox = NULL,   area_ha_min = NULL,   table = "whse_basemapping.fwa_lakes_poly",   cols = c("waterbody_poly_id", "waterbody_key", "waterbody_type", "area_ha",     "gnis_name_1", "blue_line_key", "watershed_group_code", "geom"),   limit = NULL,   ... )

title: Fetch FWA Lakes — frs_lake_fetch
text: watershed_group_code Character. Watershed group code (e.g. "BULK"). Default NULL. blue_line_key Integer. Blue line key lakes specific stream. Default NULL. bbox Numeric vector length 4 (xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax) BC Albers (EPSG:3005). Default NULL. area_ha_min Numeric. Minimum lake area hectares. Default NULL. table Character. Fully qualified table name. Default "whse_basemapping.fwa_lakes_poly". cols Character vector column names select. Default includes commonly used FWA lake attributes. limit Integer. Maximum rows return. Default NULL (limit). ... Additional arguments passed frs_db_conn().

title: Fetch FWA Lakes — frs_lake_fetch
text: sf data frame lake polygons.

title: Fetch FWA Lakes — frs_lake_fetch
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # All lakes in the Bulkley watershed group lakes <- frs_lake_fetch(watershed_group_code = "BULK")  # Lakes larger than 10 ha lakes <- frs_lake_fetch(watershed_group_code = "BULK", area_ha_min = 10) } # }

what: Query Multiple Tables Upstream or Downstream of a Network Position — frs_network
title: Query Multiple Tables Upstream or Downstream of a Network Position — frs_network
text: Point position FWA stream network retrieve features one tables — streams, crossings, barriers, fish observations, lakes, wetlands, table ltree watershed codes. Tables localcode_ltree queried directly via fwa_upstream() / fwa_downstream(). Tables without (like fwa_lakes_poly) queried via waterbody_key bridge stream network.

title: Query Multiple Tables Upstream or Downstream of a Network Position — frs_network
code: frs_network(   blue_line_key,   downstream_route_measure,   upstream_measure = NULL,   tables = NULL,   direction = "upstream",   ... )

title: Query Multiple Tables Upstream or Downstream of a Network Position — frs_network
text: blue_line_key Integer. Blue line key reference point. downstream_route_measure Numeric. Downstream route measure downstream boundary. upstream_measure Numeric NULL. Downstream route measure upstream boundary. provided, returns features two measures (network subtraction). valid direction = "upstream". tables named list table specifications. element can : character string (table name) — uses default columns list : table, cols, wscode_col, localcode_col, extra_where NULL (default), queries FWA streams . direction Character. "upstream" (default) "downstream". ... Additional arguments passed frs_db_conn().

title: Query Multiple Tables Upstream or Downstream of a Network Position — frs_network
text: named list sf data frames (plain data frames tables without geometry). one table queried, returns data frame directly.

title: Query Multiple Tables Upstream or Downstream of a Network Position — frs_network
text: upstream_measure provided, returns features two measures blue line key — network subtraction (upstream minus upstream B) spatial clipping needed.

title: Query Multiple Tables Upstream or Downstream of a Network Position — frs_network
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ blk <- 360873822  # Everything upstream of a point streams <- frs_network(blk, 166030)  # Between two points (subbasin): upstream of Byman minus upstream of Ailport result <- frs_network(blk, 208877, upstream_measure = 233564, tables = list(   streams = "whse_basemapping.fwa_stream_networks_sp",   lakes = "whse_basemapping.fwa_lakes_poly",   crossings = "bcfishpass.crossings",   observations = list(     table = "bcfishpass.observations_vw",     wscode_col = "wscode",     localcode_col = "localcode"   ) )) } # }

what: Get Stream Segments Downstream of a Network Position — frs_network_downstream
title: Get Stream Segments Downstream of a Network Position — frs_network_downstream
text: Returns FWA stream segments downstream given blue line key downstream route measure. Uses fwapg fwa_downstream() ltree comparison.

title: Get Stream Segments Downstream of a Network Position — frs_network_downstream
code: frs_network_downstream(   blue_line_key,   downstream_route_measure,   table = "whse_basemapping.fwa_stream_networks_sp",   cols = c("linear_feature_id", "blue_line_key", "waterbody_key", "edge_type",     "gnis_name", "stream_order", "stream_magnitude", "gradient",     "downstream_route_measure", "upstream_route_measure", "length_metre",     "watershed_group_code", "wscode_ltree", "localcode_ltree", "geom"),   wscode_col = "wscode_ltree",   localcode_col = "localcode_ltree",   ... )

title: Get Stream Segments Downstream of a Network Position — frs_network_downstream
text: blue_line_key Integer. Blue line key reference point. downstream_route_measure Numeric. Downstream route measure reference point. table Character. Fully qualified table name. Default "whse_basemapping.fwa_stream_networks_sp". cols Character vector column names select. Default includes commonly used FWA stream attributes. wscode_col Character. Name watershed code ltree column. Default "wscode_ltree". Use "wscode" bcfishpass views. localcode_col Character. Name local code ltree column. Default "localcode_ltree". Use "localcode" bcfishpass views. ... Additional arguments passed frs_db_conn().

title: Get Stream Segments Downstream of a Network Position — frs_network_downstream
text: sf data frame downstream stream segments.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Get all streams downstream of a point downstream <- frs_network_downstream(   blue_line_key = 360873822,   downstream_route_measure = 166030 ) } # }

title: Get Pruned Upstream Network — frs_network_prune
text: Like frs_network_upstream() additional filters: minimum stream order, maximum gradient, watershed group restriction. Filtering happens SQL efficiency.

title: Get Pruned Upstream Network — frs_network_prune
code: frs_network_prune(   blue_line_key,   downstream_route_measure,   stream_order_min = NULL,   gradient_max = NULL,   watershed_group_code = NULL,   extra_where = NULL,   table = "whse_basemapping.fwa_stream_networks_sp",   cols = c("linear_feature_id", "blue_line_key", "waterbody_key", "edge_type",     "gnis_name", "stream_order", "stream_magnitude", "gradient",     "downstream_route_measure", "upstream_route_measure", "length_metre",     "watershed_group_code", "wscode_ltree", "localcode_ltree", "geom"),   wscode_col = "wscode_ltree",   localcode_col = "localcode_ltree",   ... )

title: Get Pruned Upstream Network — frs_network_prune
text: blue_line_key Integer. Blue line key reference point. downstream_route_measure Numeric. Downstream route measure reference point. stream_order_min Integer. Minimum Strahler stream order. Default NULL. gradient_max Numeric. Maximum gradient (rise/run). Default NULL. watershed_group_code Character. Restrict watershed group. Default NULL. extra_where Character vector additional SQL predicates (applied alias s). Default NULL. table Character. Fully qualified table name. Default "whse_basemapping.fwa_stream_networks_sp". cols Character vector column names select. Default includes commonly used FWA stream attributes. wscode_col Character. Name watershed code ltree column. Default "wscode_ltree". Use "wscode" bcfishpass views. localcode_col Character. Name local code ltree column. Default "localcode_ltree". Use "localcode" bcfishpass views. ... Additional arguments passed frs_db_conn().

title: Get Pruned Upstream Network — frs_network_prune
text: sf data frame filtered upstream stream segments.

title: Get Pruned Upstream Network — frs_network_prune
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Upstream network from FWA base table, order >= 3 pruned <- frs_network_prune(   blue_line_key = 360873822,   downstream_route_measure = 166030,   stream_order_min = 3,   gradient_max = 0.05 )  # Coho rearing/spawning upstream of Neexdzii Kwa confluence co_habitat <- frs_network_prune(   blue_line_key = 360873822,   downstream_route_measure = 166030.4,   stream_order_min = 4,   watershed_group_code = "BULK",   extra_where = "(s.rearing > 0 OR s.spawning > 0)",   table = "bcfishpass.streams_co_vw",   cols = c("segmented_stream_id", "blue_line_key", "waterbody_key",            "gnis_name", "stream_order", "channel_width", "mapping_code",            "rearing", "spawning", "access", "geom"),   wscode_col = "wscode",   localcode_col = "localcode" ) } # }

what: Get Stream Segments Upstream of a Network Position — frs_network_upstream
title: Get Stream Segments Upstream of a Network Position — frs_network_upstream
text: Returns FWA stream segments upstream given blue line key downstream route measure. Uses fwapg fwa_upstream() ltree comparison.

title: Get Stream Segments Upstream of a Network Position — frs_network_upstream
code: frs_network_upstream(   blue_line_key,   downstream_route_measure,   table = "whse_basemapping.fwa_stream_networks_sp",   cols = c("linear_feature_id", "blue_line_key", "waterbody_key", "edge_type",     "gnis_name", "stream_order", "stream_magnitude", "gradient",     "downstream_route_measure", "upstream_route_measure", "length_metre",     "watershed_group_code", "wscode_ltree", "localcode_ltree", "geom"),   wscode_col = "wscode_ltree",   localcode_col = "localcode_ltree",   ... )

title: Get Stream Segments Upstream of a Network Position — frs_network_upstream
text: blue_line_key Integer. Blue line key reference point. downstream_route_measure Numeric. Downstream route measure reference point. table Character. Fully qualified table name. Default "whse_basemapping.fwa_stream_networks_sp". cols Character vector column names select. Default includes commonly used FWA stream attributes. wscode_col Character. Name watershed code ltree column. Default "wscode_ltree". Use "wscode" bcfishpass views. localcode_col Character. Name local code ltree column. Default "localcode_ltree". Use "localcode" bcfishpass views. ... Additional arguments passed frs_db_conn().

title: Get Stream Segments Upstream of a Network Position — frs_network_upstream
text: sf data frame upstream stream segments.

title: Get Stream Segments Upstream of a Network Position — frs_network_upstream
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Get all streams upstream of a point on the Bulkley upstream <- frs_network_upstream(   blue_line_key = 360873822,   downstream_route_measure = 166030 )  # Use bcfishpass coho view upstream <- frs_network_upstream(   blue_line_key = 360873822,   downstream_route_measure = 166030,   table = "bcfishpass.streams_co_vw",   wscode_col = "wscode",   localcode_col = "localcode" ) } # }

what: Filter Stream Segments by Strahler Order — frs_order_filter
title: Filter Stream Segments by Strahler Order — frs_order_filter
text: Simple filter sf data frame stream segments. Keeps rows stream_order >= min_order.

title: Filter Stream Segments by Strahler Order — frs_order_filter
code: frs_order_filter(streams, min_order)

title: Filter Stream Segments by Strahler Order — frs_order_filter
text: streams sf data frame stream_order column (e.g. frs_stream_fetch() frs_network_upstream()). min_order Integer. Minimum Strahler stream order keep.

title: Filter Stream Segments by Strahler Order — frs_order_filter
text: sf data frame low-order streams removed.

title: Filter Stream Segments by Strahler Order — frs_order_filter
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ streams <- frs_stream_fetch(watershed_group_code = "BULK") big_streams <- frs_order_filter(streams, min_order = 4) } # }

what: Locate a Point on the FWA Stream Network — frs_point_locate
title: Locate a Point on the FWA Stream Network — frs_point_locate
text: Given blue line key downstream route measure, return point geometry stream network. Wraps fwapg fwa_locatealong().

title: Locate a Point on the FWA Stream Network — frs_point_locate
code: frs_point_locate(blue_line_key, downstream_route_measure, ...)

title: Locate a Point on the FWA Stream Network — frs_point_locate
text: blue_line_key Integer. Blue line key stream. downstream_route_measure Numeric. Downstream route measure metres. ... Additional arguments passed frs_db_conn().

title: Locate a Point on the FWA Stream Network — frs_point_locate
text: sf data frame single point geometry.

title: Locate a Point on the FWA Stream Network — frs_point_locate
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Get the point at measure 1000 on a stream pt <- frs_point_locate(blue_line_key = 360873822, downstream_route_measure = 1000) } # }

what: Snap a Point to the Nearest FWA Stream — frs_point_snap
title: Snap a Point to the Nearest FWA Stream — frs_point_snap
text: Wraps fwapg fwa_indexpoint() function snap x/y coordinates nearest stream segment. Returns snapped point blue line key, downstream route measure, distance stream.

title: Snap a Point to the Nearest FWA Stream — frs_point_snap
code: frs_point_snap(x, y, srid = 4326L, tolerance = 5000, num_features = 1L, ...)

title: Snap a Point to the Nearest FWA Stream — frs_point_snap
text: x Numeric. Longitude easting. y Numeric. Latitude northing. srid Integer. Spatial reference ID input coordinates. Default 4326 (WGS84 lon/lat). tolerance Numeric. Maximum search distance metres. Default 5000. num_features Integer. Number candidate matches return. Default 1. ... Additional arguments passed frs_db_conn().

title: Snap a Point to the Nearest FWA Stream — frs_point_snap
text: sf data frame columns: linear_feature_id, gnis_name, blue_line_key, downstream_route_measure, distance_to_stream, snapped point geom.

title: Snap a Point to the Nearest FWA Stream — frs_point_snap
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Snap a lon/lat point to the nearest stream snapped <- frs_point_snap(x = -126.5, y = 54.5) snapped$blue_line_key snapped$downstream_route_measure } # }

what: Fetch FWA Stream Network Segments — frs_stream_fetch
title: Fetch FWA Stream Network Segments — frs_stream_fetch
text: Retrieve stream segments stream network table. Filter watershed group code, blue line key, /bounding box.

title: Fetch FWA Stream Network Segments — frs_stream_fetch
code: frs_stream_fetch(   watershed_group_code = NULL,   blue_line_key = NULL,   bbox = NULL,   stream_order_min = NULL,   table = "whse_basemapping.fwa_stream_networks_sp",   cols = c("linear_feature_id", "blue_line_key", "waterbody_key", "edge_type",     "gnis_name", "stream_order", "stream_magnitude", "gradient",     "downstream_route_measure", "upstream_route_measure", "length_metre",     "watershed_group_code", "wscode_ltree", "localcode_ltree", "geom"),   limit = NULL,   ... )

title: Fetch FWA Stream Network Segments — frs_stream_fetch
text: watershed_group_code Character. Watershed group code (e.g. "BULK"). Default NULL. blue_line_key Integer. Blue line key specific stream. Default NULL. bbox Numeric vector length 4 (xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax) BC Albers (EPSG:3005). Default NULL. stream_order_min Integer. Minimum Strahler stream order return. Default NULL (orders). table Character. Fully qualified table name. Default "whse_basemapping.fwa_stream_networks_sp". cols Character vector column names select. Default includes commonly used FWA stream attributes. limit Integer. Maximum rows return. Default NULL (limit). ... Additional arguments passed frs_db_conn().

title: Fetch FWA Stream Network Segments — frs_stream_fetch
text: sf data frame stream segments.

title: Fetch FWA Stream Network Segments — frs_stream_fetch
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # All streams in the Bulkley watershed group streams <- frs_stream_fetch(watershed_group_code = "BULK")  # Streams with order >= 4 streams <- frs_stream_fetch(watershed_group_code = "BULK", stream_order_min = 4)  # Custom columns and table streams <- frs_stream_fetch(   watershed_group_code = "BULK",   cols = c("blue_line_key", "gnis_name", "stream_order", "geom") ) } # }

what: Get Waterbody Polygons Upstream or Downstream of a Network Position — frs_waterbody_network
title: Get Waterbody Polygons Upstream or Downstream of a Network Position — frs_waterbody_network
text: Returns lake wetland polygons upstream downstream given blue line key downstream route measure. Polygon tables (fwa_lakes_poly, fwa_wetlands_poly) NULL localcode_ltree, fwa_upstream() / fwa_downstream() query directly. function bridges stream network: runs traversal stream segments (localcode_ltree), extracts distinct waterbody_key values, joins polygon table.

title: Get Waterbody Polygons Upstream or Downstream of a Network Position — frs_waterbody_network
code: frs_waterbody_network(   blue_line_key,   downstream_route_measure,   table = "whse_basemapping.fwa_lakes_poly",   cols = c("waterbody_key", "waterbody_type", "gnis_name_1", "area_ha", "blue_line_key",     "watershed_group_code", "geom"),   direction = "upstream",   ... )

title: Get Waterbody Polygons Upstream or Downstream of a Network Position — frs_waterbody_network
text: blue_line_key Integer. Blue line key reference point. downstream_route_measure Numeric. Downstream route measure reference point. table Character. Fully qualified polygon table name. Default "whse_basemapping.fwa_lakes_poly". cols Character vector column names select polygon table. Default includes commonly used attributes. direction Character. "upstream" (default) "downstream". ... Additional arguments passed frs_db_conn().

title: Get Waterbody Polygons Upstream or Downstream of a Network Position — frs_waterbody_network
text: sf data frame waterbody polygons.

title: Get Waterbody Polygons Upstream or Downstream of a Network Position — frs_waterbody_network
text: See fresh#8 background.

title: Get Waterbody Polygons Upstream or Downstream of a Network Position — frs_waterbody_network
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Upstream lakes from the Neexdzii Kwa / Wedzin Kwa confluence lakes <- frs_waterbody_network(   blue_line_key = 360873822,   downstream_route_measure = 166030 )  # Upstream wetlands wetlands <- frs_waterbody_network(   blue_line_key = 360873822,   downstream_route_measure = 166030,   table = "whse_basemapping.fwa_wetlands_poly" )  # Downstream lakes lakes_ds <- frs_waterbody_network(   blue_line_key = 360873822,   downstream_route_measure = 166030,   direction = "downstream" ) } # }

title: Fetch FWA Wetlands — frs_wetland_fetch

title: Fetch FWA Wetlands — frs_wetland_fetch
code: frs_wetland_fetch(   watershed_group_code = NULL,   blue_line_key = NULL,   bbox = NULL,   area_ha_min = NULL,   table = "whse_basemapping.fwa_wetlands_poly",   cols = c("waterbody_poly_id", "waterbody_key", "waterbody_type", "area_ha",     "gnis_name_1", "blue_line_key", "watershed_group_code", "geom"),   limit = NULL,   ... )

title: Fetch FWA Wetlands — frs_wetland_fetch
text: watershed_group_code Character. Watershed group code (e.g. "BULK"). Default NULL. blue_line_key Integer. Blue line key wetlands specific stream. Default NULL. bbox Numeric vector length 4 (xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax) BC Albers (EPSG:3005). Default NULL. area_ha_min Numeric. Minimum wetland area hectares. Default NULL. table Character. Fully qualified table name. Default "whse_basemapping.fwa_wetlands_poly". cols Character vector column names select. Default includes commonly used FWA wetland attributes. limit Integer. Maximum rows return. Default NULL (limit). ... Additional arguments passed frs_db_conn().

title: Fetch FWA Wetlands — frs_wetland_fetch
text: sf data frame wetland polygons.

title: Fetch FWA Wetlands — frs_wetland_fetch
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # All wetlands in the Bulkley watershed group wetlands <- frs_wetland_fetch(watershed_group_code = "BULK")  # Wetlands larger than 5 ha wetlands <- frs_wetland_fetch(watershed_group_code = "BULK", area_ha_min = 5) } # }

title: fresh 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2019-10-21 Initial release. Stream network-aware spatial operations via direct SQL fwapg bcfishpass. See function reference details.
